items.title: Savannah Guthrie sets Today show return after mother's disappearance
items.summary_auto: After a two-month absence sparked by her 84-year-old mother's apparent abduction, Savannah Guthrie will return to NBC's Today show next month,  saying in an interview that aired Friday  'joy will be my protest.'

items.title: North American stocks fall, oil prices rise as Trump's delays fail to quell fears
items.summary_auto: The S&P 500, Nasdaq and Dow Jones Industrial Average all sank in early trading, a day after Wall Street's worst drop since the conflict with Iran started.

items.title: Judge temporarily blocks Pentagon's blacklist of AI company Anthropic
items.summary_auto: In their lawsuit, Anthropic accused the government of violating its rights when U.S. Secretary of War Pete Hegseth designated Anthropic a national security supply-chain risk. The judge's decision is the latest in the saga between the AI company and the government over how Anthropic's technology can be used for defence purposes.

items.title: 'This is not murder': Defence wraps arguments at trial for Ontario couple accused in case involving 2 brothers
2 humanitarian aid boats en route to Cuba are missing, Mexico says
items.summary_auto: Mexico's navy said on Thursday it had activated a search-and-rescue operation in the Caribbean to locate two sailboats carrying humanitarian aid to Cuba after the vessels failed to arrive in Havana as scheduled.

items.title: Judge hands defeat to Musk's X, throws out lawsuit accusing advertisters of illegal boycott
items.summary_auto: A U.S. judge on Thursday dismissed X Corp.'s antitrust lawsuit that accused the World Federation of Advertisers ‌and major companies including Mars, CVS Health and Colgate-Palmolive of illegally boycotting billionaire entrepreneur Elon Musk’s social media company.

items.title: Kingsbury's 'last dance': Canadian freestyle legend confirms he'll end his career with last race in Quebec
items.summary_auto: The five-time Olympic medallist confirmed he’s retiring at the end of competition this weekend at the Canadian freestyle ski championships at his home course in Quebec.

items.title: 'Massive overdose' of sedative led to man's cardiac arrest, brain injury at Winnipeg hospital: lawsuit
items.summary_auto: The Alberta government and the father of a man who suffered cardiac arrest and permanent brain damage after he was allegedly given a "massive overdose" of a potent sedative at a Winnipeg hospital two years ago are suing the facility, three medical providers and two Manitoba health authorities for negligence.

items.title: Israel says it will expand attacks on Iran as diplomats push for end to war
items.summary_auto: Israel's defence minister said on Friday that the military's attacks on Iran "will escalate and expand," as the crisis was the subject of a UN Security Council meeting and a prevailing topic near Paris as G7 foreign ministers met.

items.title: Canada loaning millions to proposed Nunavik rare earth mining project linked to Trump White House
items.summary_auto: Amid Prime Minister Mark Carney’s calls that the Canada-U.S. relationship is ruptured, Ottawa has committed $175-million to a mining project in northern Quebec whose major U.S. investor is closely linked to the Trump administration.

items.title: 6 key moments from the Supreme Court challenge of Quebec's secularism law
items.summary_auto: Four days of hearings in the Supreme Court challenge to Quebec’s secularism law, Bill 21, are over. While the hearings dealt mostly with complex legal questions, there were some moments that revealed the intensity and passionate nature of the debates.

items.title: Why OpenAI is ditching Sora, its generative video app
items.summary_auto: OpenAI has decided to scrap Sora, its app that generated videos based on user prompts. The move reveals that the company, once the disruptor-in-chief of the AI world, is now facing something of an identity crisis as it struggles to pick a lane on the busy road to profitability.

items.title: Blue Jays open 50th season: How the team went from fledgling franchise to mature contender
items.summary_auto: As they begin their 50th season in Major League Baseball, the Toronto Blue Jays are facing some high expectations after coming close to winning the 2025 World Series. Here's a look at the team's journey over the years.

items.title: At historic low point, New Democrats descend on Winnipeg to choose a new leader
items.summary_auto: The NDP will have a new leader by Sunday afternoon — and whoever comes out of the party's convention in Winnipeg with the top job will be tasked with bringing the party back from a historic low.

items.title: 'It's absurd; it's brain rot': Welcome to AI fruit slop, where fruits cheat on each other
items.summary_auto: AI "fruit slop" videos are racking up millions of views on TikTok and Instagram, and generating reaction and parody videos from users hooked on juicy storylines centred around romance and betrayal. Think of it like smartphone soap operas — only the stars are lustful, anthropomorphic strawberries and bananas.

items.title: Transport Canada warned about WestJet seating 'hazard' weeks before viral video: documents
items.summary_auto: A WestJet flight attendant alerted the federal government to an "imminent safety risk" and called for a "prompt review" after a passenger on a flight to Calgary in November became 'stuck in his seat' on a reconfigured plane that had less legroom, documents obtained by CBC News show.

items.title: Sora, OpenAI's generative video app, is history. Here's what that means for the firm's future
items.summary_auto: OpenAI has decided to scrap Sora, its app that generated videos based on user prompts. The move reveals that the company, once the disruptor-in-chief of the AI world, is now facing something of an identity crisis as it struggles to pick a lane on the busy road to profitability.

items.title: What's new in Ottawa-Alberta deal to cut methane emissions from oil and gas sector
items.summary_auto: As part of the wide-ranging Canada-Alberta memorandum of understanding on energy policy and a new oil pipeline, the two sides announced an agreement-in-principle on new methane rules and targets. It could speed up methane reductions, experts say, but some questions remain.

items.title: Ontario court slashing $500M legal fees will have ‘chilling effect’ on other cases, argue lawyers
items.summary_auto: Lawyers who had over $500 million in legal fees struck down by an Ontario court are appealing the decision, arguing the case could have wider implications affecting access to justice.

items.title: The Junos are adding a category for Latin music. Musicians and fans say it's been a long time coming
items.summary_auto: The Juno Awards' new Latin music recording of the year category means to reflect the genre's stratospheric popularity, musicians and industry insiders say.

items.title: How the Blue Jays went from fledgling franchise to mature World Series contender
items.summary_auto: As they begin their 50th season in Major League Baseball, the Toronto Blue Jays are facing some high expectations after coming close to winning the 2025 World Series. Here's a look at the team's journey over the years.

items.title: 'It only takes one' — Why a runway collision like LaGuardia could happen in Canada
items.summary_auto: The Transportation Safety Board has been sounding the alarm on the risk of collision due to runway incursions — the incorrect presence of a vehicle, person or aircraft on a surface designated for takeoff or landing — since 2010.

items.title: TheMoment a bear cub was saved by B.C. volunteers
items.summary_auto: Angelika Langen, co-founder of the Northern Lights Wildlife Society, tells The National about the moment her shelter rescued an abandoned bear cub near Kamloops, B.C.

items.title: Susan Bonner signs off after 40 years with CBC News
items.summary_auto: After four decades guiding Canadians through major news stories, CBC’s Susan Bonner has signed off for the last time as host of Your World Tonight. Bonner’s extensive journalism career took her across the country and around the world, including reporting for The National.

items.title: Trump says he'll sign an order to get airport security workers paid, but gave no further details
At Issue | Canada hits its NATO targets. What comes next?
items.summary_auto: At Issue this week: The benefits, and challenges, of Canada finally meeting its two per cent NATO spending target. The uphill political battle facing the next NDP Leader. And, the growing provincial divide over how judges are appointed in Canada.

items.title: Can the Blue Jays go all the way this year?
items.summary_auto: CBC Sports' daily newsletter looks at the 2026 Toronto Blue Jays, who open a new season on Friday night hoping to build on their surprising run to the World Series.

items.title: Wall Street sees worst drop since Iran war began, as Nasdaq sinks 10% below its record
items.summary_auto: Stocks fell sharply on Thursday, and oil prices rose as doubt took over from hope on Wall Street about a possible end to the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran.

items.title: Judge in Maduro trial refuses to dismiss charges
items.summary_auto: Nicolás Maduro, president of Venezuela until he was captured in a U.S. raid, appeared in a New York court with his wife on Thursday. Their lawyers have argued the case should be dismissed as sanctions on Venezuela prevent the Maduros from paying legal fees.

items.title: Heat your home with oil? 'Sky high' bills a growing struggle in N.L.
items.summary_auto: People who heat their home with oil are feeling the burn, as fuel prices continue to tick upwards in Newfoundland and Labrador.

items.title: Bodies of pilots who died in collision at LaGuardia Airport repatriated to Canada
items.summary_auto: The bodies of the two Air Canada pilots who died in a collision on a runway at LaGuardia Airport on Sunday have returned to Canada.

items.title: Ontario budget boosting home care funding, but unlikely to reach long-term care bed goal
items.summary_auto: Ontario is investing $1.1 billion more in home health care as it grapples with the reality that it is unlikely to achieve its goal of building 58,000 new long-term care beds by 2028.

items.title: Death of First Nation chief's grandson in house fire spurs renewed calls for Ottawa to take action
items.summary_auto: The recent death of the three-year-old grandson of Kitchenuhmaykoosib Inninuwug Chief Donny Morris has prompted renewed calls for Ottawa to take action. The Oji-Cree community in northwestern Ontario and a tribal council launched a human rights complaint seven months ago against Indigenous Services Canada, arguing systemic discrimination is behind chronic underfunding of on-reserve fire services.

items.title: TD revises 2026 housing market forecast, expects sales and prices to fall this year
items.summary_auto: As of December, TD initially forecasted a 9.3 per cent year-over-year gain in home sales for 2026 as well as a 4.1 per cent increase in average home prices. The report says pent-up demand is taking longer than expected to return, but projects sales will rebound in 2027.
